water.title: Smart Farming Advice
water.subtitle: Smart irrigation and nutrient recommendations for your crops.
water.irrigationLow: Soil moisture is below the optimal range. Irrigation is recommended today.
water.irrigationOptimal: Soil moisture is currently optimal. No irrigation needed for next 24 hours.
water.nitrogenLow: Nitrogen level is low during vegetative stage. Apply nitrogen fertilizer to support leaf growth.
water.nutrientStable: Nutrient levels are stable. Maintain current fertigation schedule.

weather.subtitle: 7-day forecast and farming advisories.
weather.alertDesc: Heavy rain is expected on Wednesday 18th. We recommend halting irrigation and ensuring localized drainage systems are clear. Avoid applying fertilizers 24 hours prior.
weather.autoRescheduleDesc: Events are adjusted in real-time based on high rain and wind forecasts.
weather.rainFertDelay: Delay recommended due to potential runoff.
weather.windHighDrift: High wind speed detected. Risk of drift.
weather.efficientWindows: here are the most efficient windows to reduce evaporation and heat stress.

messages.soilDryTemp: Water your crop today. Soil is dry and temperature is high.

community.description: Share crop updates, disease alerts, and learn from nearby farmers
community.title: Farmer Community

rust.cause: Leaf Rust is commonly caused by fungal pathogens that spread rapidly under humid and warm conditions.
rust.treatment: Apply fungicides such as Propiconazole or Mancozeb at recommended dosage.
rust.prevention: Use resistant crop varieties, maintain proper spacing, and avoid excess nitrogen fertilization.
rust.fertilizer: Balanced NPK fertilization is recommended to maintain plant immunity.

blast.cause: Blast Disease is caused by Magnaporthe oryzae, flourishing in humid and rainy weather.
blast.treatment: Use Tricyclazole or Isoprothiolane immediately upon spotting lesions.
blast.prevention: Optimize planting time to avoid rainy, humid conditions during heading stage.
blast.fertilizer: Avoid excessive nitrogen application which increases susceptibility.

aphids.cause: Aphid infestation occurs rapidly in dry and warm climates, stunting plant growth.
aphids.treatment: Apply Neem oil spray or systemic insecticides like Imidacloprid.
aphids.prevention: Encourage natural predators like ladybugs and use reflective mulches.
aphids.fertilizer: Avoid over-fertilizing with nitrogen, which promotes lush growth attractive to aphids.

recommendations.noRecs: No recommendations for this priority level.

schemes.title: Government Agricultural Schemes
schemes.subtitle: Explore government schemes, subsidies, and support programs available for farmers.

list.name: PM-KISAN Samman Nidhi
list.shortDesc: Income support scheme providing financial assistance to small and marginal farmers.
list.eligibility: Small and marginal landholding farmers. Family owning cultivable land up to 2 hectares.
list.benefits: ₹6000 per year financial support in three equal installments.

list.name: Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana (PMFBY)
list.shortDesc: Crop insurance scheme providing financial support to farmers suffering crop loss/damage.
list.eligibility: All farmers growing notified crops in notified areas, including sharecroppers and tenant farmers.
list.benefits: Comprehensive risk cover from pre-sowing to post-harvest losses. Premium is extremely low (1.5% to 2%).
steps: Register on National Crop Insurance Portal
Submit crop details and pay premium before cut-off date

list.name: PM Krishi Sinchai Yojana
list.shortDesc: Scheme to improve farm water use efficiency and expand cultivable area under assured irrigation.
list.eligibility: Farmers, Water User Associations, Co-operative Societies.
list.benefits: Subsidies for micro-irrigation systems like drip and sprinkler irrigation (up to 55%).

list.name: Soil Health Card Scheme
list.shortDesc: Provides soil health cards to farmers with crop-wise recommendations of nutrients and fertilizers.
list.benefits: Free soil testing and customized fertilizer usage advice to improve yield and save costs.

list.name: Kisan Credit Card (KCC)
list.shortDesc: Meets the working capital and short-term credit requirements of farmers.
list.eligibility: Individual/Joint borrowers who are owner cultivators, tenant farmers, output sharecroppers.
list.benefits: Access to short-term crop loans at concessional interest rates (subvention up to 3%).

list.name: National Agriculture Market (e-NAM)
list.shortDesc: Pan-India electronic trading portal networking existing APMC mandis.
list.benefits: Direct access to buyers across India, ensuring better price realization for produce.
steps: Register on e-NAM portal or mobile app
Bring produce to mandi for assaying and online bidding
